title: Thermistor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The dryer's thermistor temperature sensor has an open circuit, preventing temperature monitoring.
fullDescription: The E1 error code indicates the dryer's main thermistor (NTC temperature sensor) has an open circuit. The control board cannot read the drum temperature, so it shuts down the heater as a safety precaution. This is typically caused by a failed thermistor, a disconnected wiring harness, or a corroded connector. The dryer may run without heat or refuse to start.

causes.cause: Disconnected or loose thermistor wiring connector
Corroded wiring harness at the thermistor
Main control board temperature input circuit failure

symptoms: Dryer runs but produces no heat
E1 displayed on the control panel
Dryer stops shortly after starting
Clothes come out damp after a full cycle
Dryer starts heating then suddenly stops

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 2 minutes, then plug it back in. This clears temporary sensor glitches that may have triggered E1.

fixSteps.title: Check thermistor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove the rear panel to access the thermistor, typically located on the blower housing or inside the drum housing. Check that the wire connector is firmly seated. Look for signs of melting, corrosion, or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor leads and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (25C), it should read approximately 10,000 to 12,000 ohms. An open circuit (infinite resistance) confirms a failed sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting clip or screw holding the thermistor. Install the new sensor in the same position. Reconnect the wiring harness and reassemble the panels. Test the dryer on a timed heat cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor and wiring test fine and the control board temperature circuit may be at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E1 thermistor error fix

faq.q: Can I run the dryer without fixing E1?
faq.a: The dryer may run without heat, but the safety circuit prevents proper heating. Running without fixing E1 wastes energy and does not dry clothes properly.

faq.q: How much does a dryer thermistor cost?
faq.a: Haier dryer thermistors typically cost $8 to $22 and are one of the least expensive dryer repairs.

faq.q: Where is the thermistor located in a Haier dryer?
faq.a: The thermistor is typically located on the blower housing or inside the drum housing area, accessible by removing the rear panel.

title: Thermistor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The dryer thermistor has a short circuit, reading abnormally low resistance.
fullDescription: The E2 error code indicates the dryer thermistor is reading abnormally low resistance, indicating a short circuit in the sensor or its wiring. This causes the control board to read an unrealistically high temperature, shutting down the heater immediately. The dryer will not heat or will display E2 and refuse to start a heated cycle.

causes.cause: Moisture intrusion on the thermistor connector

symptoms: E2 displayed immediately when heated cycle starts
Dryer runs but with no heat
Dryer shuts down within seconds of starting
Display may show unrealistically high temperature reading
Intermittent E2 that comes and goes

fixSteps.title: Unplug and reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes to reset the control board. Condensation or temporary moisture on the sensor can cause false short readings.

fixSteps.title: Inspect thermistor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and access the thermistor. Look for pinched wires where the harness may be caught under a panel or against a sharp edge. Also check the connector for moisture or green corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read 10,000-12,000 ohms. A very low reading (near 0 ohms) confirms a short circuit in the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty sensor and install a new one. Route the wiring carefully to avoid pinching when reassembling the panels.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the thermistor does not resolve the issue, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E2 thermistor short circuit fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E1 and E2?
faq.a: E1 indicates an open circuit (no connection) in the thermistor, while E2 indicates a short circuit (connection with near-zero resistance). Both mean the sensor has failed but in different ways.

faq.q: Can moisture cause E2?
faq.a: Yes, condensation or moisture on the thermistor connector pins can create a temporary short circuit. Allow the dryer area to dry and reset the machine.

faq.q: Is E2 a fire hazard?
faq.a: No, E2 is actually a safety response. The control board detects what it thinks is extremely high temperature and shuts off the heater to prevent overheating.

title: Exhaust Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The exhaust duct temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E3 error code indicates the exhaust thermistor, which monitors the temperature of air leaving the drum through the exhaust duct, has failed. This sensor helps regulate drying temperature and detect restricted airflow. Without it, the dryer cannot properly control the heating cycle and will shut down for safety.

causes.cause: Lint buildup around the exhaust thermistor
Control board exhaust sensor circuit fault

symptoms: E3 displayed during drying cycle
Dryer overheats or does not heat at all
Auto-dry cycles do not end at the right time
Clothes come out overly hot or still damp
Burning smell from excessive heat

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter and exhaust
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the lint filter. Check the exhaust duct for lint buildup by disconnecting it from the back of the dryer and inspecting inside. A clogged exhaust can cause the exhaust thermistor to read abnormally.

fixSteps.title: Locate the exhaust thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The exhaust thermistor is typically near the exhaust outlet or blower housing. Remove the rear panel to access it.

fixSteps.title: Test the exhaust thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor wiring and test resistance. At room temperature, expect 10,000-12,000 ohms. Open circuit or near-zero readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the exhaust thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting hardware and install the replacement sensor. Reconnect wiring and reassemble. Test with a timed dry cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor tests fine and the dryer continues to overheat, indicating a possible control board or high-limit thermostat issue.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E3 exhaust thermistor error fix

faq.q: Can a clogged exhaust duct trigger E3?
faq.a: A severely clogged exhaust can cause abnormal temperature readings that may trigger E3, even if the thermistor itself is functional. Always clean the exhaust system first.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dryer exhaust duct?
faq.a: At least once a year, or more frequently if you do heavy laundry loads. Lint buildup in the exhaust is both a performance and fire safety issue.

faq.q: What happens if the exhaust thermistor fails while drying?
faq.a: The dryer will shut down the heater to prevent overheating. Your clothes may come out damp, but the safety system prevents fire risk.

title: Heater Relay Fault
shortDescription: The heating element relay on the control board has failed.
fullDescription: The E4 error code indicates the control board has detected a fault with the heating relay circuit. The relay that switches the heating element on and off is either stuck open (no heat), stuck closed (continuous heat), or the feedback circuit cannot verify relay operation. This is a serious issue as a stuck-closed relay can cause overheating.

causes.cause: Failed heater relay on the control board
Wiring fault between control board and heater
Power surge damaging the relay circuit

symptoms: E4 displayed when starting a heated cycle
Dryer heats continuously without cycling off
No heat at all despite E4 not appearing until mid-cycle
Burning smell from overheated components
Dryer trips the circuit breaker

fixSteps.title: Immediately stop the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: If the dryer is overheating or producing a burning smell, unplug it immediately. A stuck relay can cause a fire hazard. Do not use the dryer until the issue is resolved.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and access the heating element. Test resistance with a multimeter — expect 8 to 20 ohms for a good element. Also test between each terminal and ground (the metal housing). Any continuity to ground indicates a short.

fixSteps.title: Inspect heater wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check all wiring from the control board to the heating element for damage, burned insulation, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the heating element and wiring test fine, the relay on the control board has failed. The entire board must be replaced. Take photos of all connections before removing the old board.

whenToCallPro: If you suspect a stuck-closed relay (continuous heating), this is a fire hazard and should be repaired by a professional immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E4 heater relay control board fix

faq.q: Is E4 a fire hazard?
faq.a: Potentially yes. If the heater relay is stuck in the closed position, the heating element runs continuously, which can cause overheating and fire. Stop using the dryer immediately.

faq.q: Can I replace just the relay on the board?
faq.a: Technically possible for someone with electronics soldering skills, but most people replace the entire control board. Board-level repair voids any warranty on the replacement.

faq.q: Why did my relay fail?
faq.a: Relays have a finite number of switching cycles. Age, power surges, and a shorted heating element can all accelerate relay failure.

title: High Temperature Limit Exceeded
shortDescription: The dryer has exceeded the maximum safe operating temperature.
fullDescription: The E5 error code indicates the dryer's high-limit thermostat or safety thermistor has detected temperatures above the safe operating threshold. The heater is shut off and the dryer may stop running entirely. This is typically caused by a blocked lint filter, restricted exhaust vent, failed cycling thermostat, or a malfunctioning blower motor.

causes.cause: Blocked or kinked exhaust vent duct
Malfunctioning blower motor or blower wheel

symptoms: E5 displayed and dryer stops heating
Clothes come out extremely hot
Burning smell during operation
Dryer exterior is very hot to the touch
Dryer shuts off before cycle completes

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint filter and clean off all accumulated lint. If the filter is clogged, this alone can cause the E5 overheating error. Wash the filter with soap and water if there is a waxy film from dryer sheets.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the exhaust duct from the dryer. Use a vent brush or vacuum to clean lint from the entire duct run to the exterior vent hood. Check the exterior vent hood for blockages from lint, bird nests, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Check the high-limit thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Locate the high-limit thermostat on the heater housing. Test for continuity with a multimeter. If it shows no continuity, it has tripped and must be replaced. Note: this is a safety fuse that does not reset.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the blower system
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the blower wheel is intact and spins freely. Look for lint buildup around the blower. A broken blower fin or seized motor reduces airflow and causes overheating.

whenToCallPro: If the vent system is clean, the thermostat has been replaced, and overheating continues, indicating a possible blower motor or control board issue.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E5 high temperature overheating fix

faq.q: Is dryer overheating dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, dryer overheating is a leading cause of house fires. If you see E5, stop using the dryer and clean the lint filter and exhaust system before continuing.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dryer exhaust vent?
faq.a: At least once per year. If you use the dryer frequently or notice longer drying times, clean it more often.

faq.q: Does the high-limit thermostat reset?
faq.a: No, the high-limit thermostat is a one-time thermal fuse. Once it trips, it must be replaced. Always address the root cause of overheating before replacing it.

title: Motor Fault
shortDescription: The dryer motor has a fault preventing normal operation.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates the dryer motor is not operating correctly. The control board has detected that the motor cannot start, is drawing excessive current, or the motor speed sensor is not providing a valid signal. This prevents the drum from turning and the blower from moving air.

causes.cause: Worn motor bearings or seized motor
Broken or slipping drive belt preventing motor rotation feedback

symptoms: Drum does not rotate
E6 appears when cycle starts
Humming sound from motor but no rotation
Burning smell from motor area
Dryer trips the circuit breaker

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and remove the top panel. Check if the drive belt is intact and properly tensioned around the drum and motor pulley. A broken belt prevents drum rotation and may confuse the motor feedback circuit.

fixSteps.title: Spin the drum by hand
fixSteps.instruction: With the top open, try spinning the drum by hand. It should rotate freely with slight resistance from the belt. Excessive resistance suggests a drum roller, bearing, or idler pulley problem.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the motor start/run capacitor. Discharge it safely (short the terminals with an insulated screwdriver). Test capacitance with a multimeter that has a capacitance function. Replace if significantly out of spec from the rated value printed on the capacitor.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and test winding resistance. Start and run windings should show resistance (typically 1-10 ohms each). No continuity or very low resistance indicates winding failure.

whenToCallPro: If the motor has failed or the capacitor replacement does not resolve the issue. Motor replacement requires significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E6 motor fault error fix replacement

faq.q: How long do dryer motors last?
faq.a: A typical dryer motor lasts 10 to 15 years with normal use. Heavy use, lint buildup around the motor, and poor ventilation can shorten motor life.

faq.q: Can a broken belt cause E6?
faq.a: Yes, on some models the motor relies on drum rotation feedback. If the belt is broken, the motor may start but the control board detects no drum rotation and triggers E6.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing a dryer motor?
faq.a: If the dryer is less than 8 years old and in good condition, motor replacement is generally cost-effective at $80-$180 for the part.

title: Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The moisture or humidity sensor is not providing valid readings.
fullDescription: The E7 error code indicates the dryer's moisture sensor bars or humidity sensor is not functioning properly. These sensors detect the moisture level of the clothes to determine when to end auto-dry cycles. A failed sensor leads to over-drying or under-drying of clothes and triggers the error code.

causes.cause: Dirty or coated moisture sensor bars
Dryer sheet residue on sensor bars

symptoms: Auto-dry cycles run too long or too short
E7 displayed during auto-dry cycles only
Clothes over-dried and stiff
Clothes still damp after auto-dry cycle completes
Timed dry cycles work fine but auto-dry does not

fixSteps.title: Clean the moisture sensor bars
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the moisture sensor bars inside the drum, usually two metal strips near the lint filter. Clean them with rubbing alcohol and a soft cloth to remove dryer sheet residue, fabric softener film, and lint buildup.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor bars
fixSteps.instruction: With the dryer unplugged, use a multimeter to test continuity between the sensor bar terminals and their wiring at the control board. The bars should show infinite resistance when dry and low resistance when bridged with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the sensor bars to the control board connector. Ensure all connections are secure and no wires are broken or frayed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the moisture sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning and testing show the sensor is faulty, remove the mounting screws and disconnect the wiring. Install the replacement sensor in the same position.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the sensors does not resolve the issue and the sensor has been replaced but E7 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E7 moisture humidity sensor fix clean

faq.q: Do dryer sheets affect the moisture sensor?
faq.a: Yes, dryer sheets and fabric softener leave a waxy residue on the moisture sensor bars over time, reducing their accuracy. Clean the bars regularly if you use dryer sheets.

faq.q: Can I use timed dry as a workaround?
faq.a: Yes, timed dry cycles do not use the moisture sensor and will work normally while you address the E7 error.

faq.q: How often should I clean moisture sensors?
faq.a: Every 3 to 6 months, or whenever you notice auto-dry cycles ending at incorrect times.

title: Door Switch Error
shortDescription: The dryer door switch is not detecting proper door closure.
fullDescription: The E8 error code indicates the dryer door switch is not registering a closed door. The dryer will not start or will stop mid-cycle if the door switch loses its signal. Common causes include a faulty door switch, worn door latch, misaligned striker, or wiring issues.
symptoms: Dryer does not start and shows E8
Dryer stops mid-cycle
Door appears closed but dryer says it is open
No click heard when door closes
Must hold door extra hard to start dryer

fixSteps.title: Close the door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open and firmly close the dryer door until you hear a distinct click. Check for laundry items caught in the door seal or latch area.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch and striker
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door latch hook and the striker on the front panel. Look for wear, bending, or damage. Adjust or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Access the door switch behind the front panel. Disconnect the switch wiring and test with a multimeter. The switch should show continuity when pressed and no continuity when released.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch tests faulty, remove it from its mounting and install the replacement. Reconnect the wiring and test the dryer.

whenToCallPro: If the switch and latch are both functional and the issue is in the wiring or control board circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E8 door switch error fix

faq.q: Can I bypass the door switch?
faq.a: Never bypass the door switch. It is a safety device that prevents the dryer from running with the door open, preventing burns and injuries from the rotating drum.

faq.q: Why does the dryer stop mid-cycle with E8?
faq.a: The door switch may be intermittently losing contact due to wear. Even slight vibration during operation can cause a worn switch to momentarily open.

faq.q: How much does a door switch replacement cost?
faq.a: The switch is $8-$20 for the part. Professional installation adds $80-$160 in labor.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between the display board and main control board.
fullDescription: The E9 error code indicates a communication breakdown between the user interface display board and the main control board. This prevents the dryer from receiving cycle selections or displaying status information. The dryer will typically be non-functional until the communication link is restored.

causes.cause: Failed main control board communication circuit

symptoms: E9 displayed or display is blank
No buttons respond on the control panel
Display shows garbled characters
Dryer powers on but cannot start any cycle
Random beeping from the control panel

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. Plug back in and check if the error clears. Temporary communication glitches can often be resolved with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove the control panel cover to access the ribbon cable connecting the display and main boards. Disconnect and firmly reseat the cable at both ends.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check both boards for visible damage — burned components, corrosion, or water stains. Check the ribbon cable for bent pins or damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If damage is visible on either board, replace the affected component. Photograph all connections before removal.

whenToCallPro: If reseating the cable does not fix the issue and a board needs replacement. A technician can test signal lines to determine which board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Haier dryer E9 communication error control board fix

faq.q: Can I still use the dryer with E9?
faq.a: No, E9 prevents any cycle selection or operation. The communication link must be restored first.

faq.q: Is E9 caused by power surges?
faq.a: Yes, power surges are a common cause of board communication failures. Using a surge protector is recommended.

faq.q: How do I know which board is bad?
faq.a: If the display is completely blank, the display board is likely at fault. If it shows E9 but buttons do nothing, the main board may be the problem. A technician can test both.
